title: Avoid defining functions with variable arguments
description: Defining functions with a variable number of arguments (varargs) can make code harder to understand and maintain, and may introduce subtle bugs. Prefer explicit parameter lists to improve code clarity and reduce the risk of misuse.
recommendation: Refactor functions to use explicit parameters instead of varargs when possible. This enhances readability and helps prevent errors related to argument handling.

checks.message: Refactor functions to use explicit parameters instead of varargs when possible. This enhances readability and helps prevent errors related to argument handling.
